items.headline: Arturs Silovs may get chance to save Pittsburgh again in Game 5
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA -- Arturs Silovs already helped save the Pittsburgh Penguins' season once.
The rookie goalie has earned a chance to do it again against the Philadelphia Flyers for Game 5 of the Eastern Conference First Round at PPG Paints Arena in Pittsburgh on Monday (7 p.m. ET; SN-PIT, NBCSP, ESPN,

items.headline: P.K. Subban's bond with Montreal still solid in 2026 playoff run
items.description:
MONTREAL -- Retired defenseman P.K. Subban was back in his first NHL home over the weekend, in a suite at raucous Bell Centre for Games 3 and 4 of the Eastern Conference First Round between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa Bay Lightning.

items.headline: Edmonton Oilers pushed to brink, running out of time to find another level
items.description: ANAHEIM -- The Edmonton Oilers are in a familiar position, sitting in a hole in the Stanley Cup Playoffs and needing a monumental effort to climb out.

items.headline: Edmonton Oilers Anaheim Ducks game 4 recap April 26 2026
items.description: ANAHEIM -- Ryan Poehling scored at 2:29 of overtime to give the Anaheim Ducks a 4-3 win against the Edmonton Oilers in Game 4 of the Western Conference First Round at Honda Center on Sunday.

items.headline: Brandon Hagel provides spark in Lightning Game 4 rally
items.description: MONTREAL -- Brandon Hagel looked up at the raucous Bell Centre crowd that was showering him with boos and broke into a mischievous smile.

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning Montreal Canadiens Game 4 recap April 26 2026
items.description: MONTREAL -- Brandon Hagel scored twice in the third period, and the Tampa Bay Lightning rallied for a 3-2 win against the Montreal Canadiens in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Bell Centre on Sunday.

items.headline: Color of Hockey: Sunny Mehta's hiring as New Jersey GM 'historic'
items.description: William Douglas has been writing The Color of Hockey blog since 2012. Douglas joined NHL.com in 2019 and writes about people of color in the sport. Today, he profiles the reaction within the South Asian hockey community to the New Jersey Devils hiring Sunny Mehta as their new general manager.
